key_insights.insight: Chronic stress significantly alters brain structure, negatively impacting decision-making and increasing anxiety.
key_insights.quote: Chronic stress literally rewires your brain. It shrinks your hippocampus, it enlarges your amygdala, it makes you more anxious and less capable of making good decisions.

key_insights.insight: True happiness stems from contentment and reducing expectations, not from constant achievement.
key_insights.quote: Happiness is not about achieving more, it's about wanting less.

key_insights.insight: Happiness can be mathematically represented as the ratio between perception of events and expectations.
key_insights.quote: Your happiness is equal to or greater than your perception of the events of your life minus your expectations of how life should be.

key_insights.insight: Recognizing that thoughts are transient and separate from one's core identity is key to managing anxiety.
key_insights.quote: Understand that your thoughts are not you. You are the observer of your thoughts.

key_insights.insight: Focusing on what is within one's control can significantly reduce stress levels.
key_insights.quote: Control what you can control. You can't control the traffic, but you can control when you leave. You can't control other people, but you can control your reactions.

key_insights.insight: Stress is a significant contributor to major health issues.
key_insights.quote: When I look at the data, 70% of heart attacks are stress-related.

actionable_advice.how_to: Write down three things you are grateful for every morning.

actionable_advice.advice: Observe your thoughts without identifying with them
actionable_advice.how_to: When anxiety arises, acknowledge it but recognize it as a fleeting thought, like a passing cloud.

actionable_advice.advice: Focus on controlling what you can
actionable_advice.how_to: Identify elements in your life that are causing stress and determine which you can influence and actively adjust them.

memorable_stories: Personal experience of the speaker after the death of their son changed their definition of happiness.
contrarian_claims: Happiness is not about achieving more, but about wanting less.
Glorifying busy-ness and wearing exhaustion as a badge of honor is detrimental to well-being.
episode_title: E203: Mo Gawdat: A WARNING about Stress, Anxiety & Depression! This Is Causing 70% Of Heart Attacks!
